evals.prompt: Use $uxl-onedpl to decide whether to use host execution policies or a SYCL device policy for sorting a vector.
evals.expected_output: Explains host versus device policy tradeoffs, checks data location and device availability, preserves ordering/stability requirements, and includes a serial reference test.

evals.prompt: Use $uxl-onedpl to debug wrong results after a device algorithm writes data that is immediately read on host.
evals.expected_output: Identifies synchronization/data lifetime as a likely issue, asks about USM/buffers/queue ownership, and proposes explicit wait or dependency handling.

evals.prompt: Use $uxl-onedpl to port a std::transform plus std::reduce sequence to oneDPL on GPU.
evals.expected_output: Defines the serial contract, checks iterator and lambda constraints, selects device policy from an owned queue, and describes correctness/performance validation.
